entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal memory layer that retains user, session, and agent states across interactions. It utilizes a token-efficient single-pass extraction algorithm and hybrid retrieval to fuse semantic, keyword, and entity-linked signals.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated 'brain' for Hermes Agent features a self-wiring knowledge graph that automatically extracts entities and relationships from conversations. It employs a local-first architecture using PGLite to combine vector retrieval with graph-based backlink ranking for deep context.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a 'retain, recall, and reflect' workflow that allows agents to learn and improve their responses over time. It provides a simple two-line LLM wrapper for automatic memory management with built-in per-user isolation via metadata filtering.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a stateful memory library that tracks the evolving relationships between users, agents, and groups through natural language querying. It supports both Python and TypeScript SDKs, providing OpenAI-compatible context formatting for seamless integration.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses structured traces and real-world evaluation to iteratively improve agent performance on complex tasks. It facilitates multi-role cooperation for strategy refinement and knowledge curation across various model providers.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: Hermes-LCM provides lossless context management by storing messages in a hierarchical DAG structure backed by SQLite. This approach allows for depth-aware context compaction while ensuring raw historical data remains available for targeted retrieval via agent tools.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project enables a private, self-hosted backend for the Honcho memory system specifically tailored for Hermes Agent persistence. It includes automated setup scripts for Docker and supports any OpenAI-compatible provider or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It utilizes a hybrid retrieval system combining BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs, accessible via MCP or agent plugins.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: ContextPilot optimizes long-context inference for Hermes and OpenClaw by deduplicating redundant blocks and reordering prefixes to boost cache hits. This results in significantly faster prefill speeds and reduced token usage without sacrificing accuracy.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML 'engram' format to facilitate zero-cost local storage for multi-agent systems. It implements ACT-R activation logic to strengthen relevant facts while allowing stale information to decay naturally.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by indexing codebases and media with automatic OCR and summarization capabilities. It uses bidirectional edge tags to create navigable relationships between notes, exposed through MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency memory system for Hermes that leverages SQLite for sub-millisecond local search and storage. It focuses on offline-capable episodic memory consolidation, providing native vector and full-text search in a lightweight package.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs to predict information needs before they arise. Built for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, it uses SQLite-vec and GGUF models for a fully local, MCP-native experience.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It enables Hermes to perform specialized tasks across 26 domains, including forensics and malware analysis, using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw introduces an 'Agentic Evolver' that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It is designed to unify skill libraries across multiple devices and sessions, offering native integration for Hermes and Codex.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI writing patterns by identifying 36 distinct structural tells. It uses a multi-tier word replacement table and a two-pass audit process to ensure text remains natural and free of recycled transitions.
entries.wondelai/skills: This cross-platform library offers modular skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy via a dedicated CLI. It is fully compatible with agentskills.io, allowing Hermes to quickly adopt professional frameworks for high-level business and design tasks.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe support for the Agent Skills open format specifically for Pydantic AI. It optimizes performance by using progressive disclosure of metadata to reduce token usage during skill discovery.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams, including UML and flowcharts, from natural language descriptions. It features an iterative review process with self-check loops and supports multi-format export via the draw.io desktop CLI.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches the agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem-solving. It helps identify structural conservation laws in codebases and generates reports detailing potential analysis blind spots.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library provides standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink oracle networks, including CCIP, VRF, and Data Feeds. It follows the agentskills.io specification to facilitate automated onboarding and runtime operations for blockchain-enabled agents.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill enables Hermes to weave prose, diagrams, and source code into unified .lit.md files. It includes a 'tangler' to extract runnable code and automatically syncs edits between source files and documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides a shared memory system via markdown-based directories, allowing Hermes to remember work across different instances. It includes an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality data to fine-tune replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry for reusable AI skills that features recursive dependency resolution and a Python SDK for lifecycle management. It uniquely integrates commerce rails, supporting skill transactions using both USD and the TON blockchain.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX image generation models. They allow Hermes to handle precise hex color specifications and structured JSON prompting for high-fidelity visual outputs.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both documentation and Python scaffolds, allowing Hermes to expand its own capabilities dynamically through slash commands.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running agents that manages mission state and structured memory within a local directory. It facilitates multi-agent collaboration through markdown briefs and provides a TUI dashboard for real-time tracking.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables autonomous execution of complex, multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints for progress tracking. It ensures reliability by providing rollback procedures for safe recovery if a task encounters an error.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading pattern to minimize token consumption during tool discovery. Its socket-based daemon architecture allows for persistent connections and hot-reloading of server configurations.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts existing code repositories and developer documentation into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant skills. It maintains high transparency by providing verifiable audit trails with file-and-line level provenance.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub serves as a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills, featuring automated security scanning with over 65 threat detection rules. It includes an agent-to-agent feedback system to establish trust and reliability scores for creators.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This C# and .NET implementation of the agentskills.io spec brings progressive disclosure and SKILL.md support to the Microsoft ecosystem. It integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI to allow seamless tool-based skill loading for .NET developers.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill enables Hermes to manage Nextcloud files, calendars, and contacts using standard WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It features a guided setup script for secure authentication via Nextcloud App Passwords.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a comprehensive collection of Nix packages for AI coding agents, including Hermes, with daily automated updates to ensure access to the latest versions. It enables unified execution via Nix flakes, making it a powerful choice for developers who prioritize package consistency and CLI-driven workflows.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security environments by implementing hardware-enforced sandboxing through NVIDIA OpenShell kernel-level security. It offers multi-layer protection with pre-configured policy presets that strictly control network egress and filesystem writes during agent execution.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository offers a NixOS module for declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services. It distinguishes itself with robust secrets handling via sops-nix and support for hybrid skill installations, catering to users who require reproducible, production-grade system deployments.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent provides a GUI and over 100 built-in tools without requiring Docker or complex setups. It supports local model integration via LM Studio and includes a dynamic tool maker for building custom automation pipelines directly on a workstation.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker sandbox image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent. It is ideal for users seeking a lightweight containerized environment that supports persistent state through mounted directories and custom builds via build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ acts as a Docker-first control plane capable of orchestrating multiple agent instances with distinct identities and workspaces from a single web application. It is unique for its hierarchy-aware delegation features and enterprise MCP access, allowing for complex multi-agent task scheduling.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting headless Ubuntu and Debian environments, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service for production use. It is specifically optimized for autonomous execution, enabling the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs without manual intervention.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment option utilizes a Docker Compose stack integrated with a ttyd web terminal for browser-based agent interaction. It is designed for easy management through the Portainer UI and leverages GitHub Actions for automated image builds and deployment.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen with zero external dependencies. It is essential for Hermes users requiring local task dispatching, cost monitoring, and human-in-the-loop sign-offs via the Aegis review system.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with native support for Hermes and over 20 other LLM providers. It distinguishes itself through durable agent memory and a visual organization chart that tracks how agents delegate tasks and learn new skills from conversations.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This specialized framework uses an automated outer-loop search process to optimize the harness code used for evaluating Hermes Agent. It orchestrates complex benchmark evaluations, such as TBLite, by mutating and comparing candidate code to find the most effective performance frontiers.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine distinct agents and provides a real-time dashboard to visualize task execution, code generation, and live scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project coordinates a pipeline of 17 specialized agents focused on research, infrastructure, and security. It ensures high-standard outputs by enforcing mandatory quality gates where reviewer and tester agents must approve work before it moves through the master orchestrator.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron automates an eight-phase software development lifecycle by combining Hermes Agent with Supermodel MCP code graphs. It maintains architectural integrity by using graph-diff phase gates to validate structural changes at every step from planning to health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This framework implements ACP-style delegation to route tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code. It focuses on operational safety by isolating delegation workflows to prevent context pollution and enforcing strict timeouts and output limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI provides local visualization for tracking token consumption and costs across multiple AI coding agents. It integrates directly with Hermes Agent alongside other platforms like Claude Code and Gemini to help developers monitor their usage metrics.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal UI through YAML-based configuration files for ASCII banners, spinners, and color schemes. It simplifies theme creation by allowing custom skins to inherit from default settings for quick visual adjustments.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution without external processes or OAuth. It specifically includes MLOps tools for GGUF inference and reinforcement learning training within the CCC environment.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control via the Model Context Protocol (MCP), featuring a visible UI for human supervision. It supports durable state management through action rollbacks and automatically infers structured schemas for web elements like forms and products.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md is a framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents that utilize per-folder configurations, local memories, and specialized toolsets. It enables developers to switch seamlessly between global and project-specific agents with custom instructions tailored to a particular directory.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static linter uses regex and heuristics to detect structural failure modes in agent configurations and system prompts without making LLM calls. It provides deterministic validation for YAML and JSON files, ensuring schema-intent alignment for Hermes deployments.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility automates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and skills into the Hermes YAML format. It handles the transfer of complex model configurations and local credentials to ensure a smooth workspace migration.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This Docker-based deployment tool automates the setup of a full Hermes Agent stack including LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for local GPU inference. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins for memory and social integration, enabling a zero-cost environment in minutes.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This mobile-responsive web interface mirrors the full Hermes Agent CLI experience through a functional three-panel layout for chat, navigation, and file browsing. It features a unique visual context ring that provides real-time monitoring of token usage during active sessions.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-channel connectivity, allowing users to configure and manage Hermes across Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp. It includes advanced usage analytics for tracking costs and model distribution alongside a web terminal for remote backend management.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: Designed for zero-fork compatibility with vanilla installations, this workspace introduces 'Swarm Mode' for dispatching multiple role-based persistent agents. The interface unifies chat, memory browsing, and terminal access into a single cohesive environment.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This self-hosted gateway acts as a command center for starting, stopping, and configuring individual agent profiles. It provides deep system visibility through an xterm.js terminal with full PTY support and detailed token analytics for cost tracking.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS application that connects directly to Hermes environments via SSH without requiring remote helper services. It organizes agent activity through a Mac-centric UI featuring Kanban boards for task management and native remote file editing.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React dashboard operates as a Telegram Mini App, offering a terminal-style chat interface with slash command support. It allows users to spawn up to five concurrent background agents while monitoring hardware metrics like CPU and disk usage.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows GUI that enables local agent execution without Docker or administrative rights, integrating with LM Studio and ComfyUI. It features a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers and comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools for media generation.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the onboarding process with a guided installer for local dependencies and environment setup. It provides a unified GUI to manage 16 different messaging gateways and 14 distinct toolsets from a single installation.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides a real-time activity feed and FTS5-powered search for inspecting full conversation histories. It is tailored for oversight, offering read-only configuration viewing and management of scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that emphasizes extensibility through an integrated marketplace for discovering skills and MCP servers. It features tool execution timelines and profile-scoped management for global memory and agent configurations.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This lightweight, glassmorphic web interface is built as a single-file React application supported by a Python proxy. It specializes in transparency, offering real-time log streaming and live task tracking alongside integrated MCP tool management.
